headline: Oil jumps +3–4% as Hormuz traffic stays near-stalled.
Shipping attacks continue as Hormuz effectively shut.
Hormuz remains effectively blocked; oil up ~4–6% on the day; Qatar LNG outage persists.
Hormuz still near-standstill (de facto disruption); oil slightly lower vs last check; Gulf states signal collective defense.
Hormuz remains de-facto shut (traffic near-halt) as war enters week 2; LNG disruption persists; oil jumps back up.
Hormuz disruption persists.
Hormuz effectively shut; Brent ~$93; gold ~$5,159; BTC ~$68.0k.
Hormuz effectively shut (tanker traffic near-zero); Brent holds ~$93 after >8% jump; LNG shock persists
Hormuz disruption persists (tanker traffic near-zero); Brent/WTI eased on latest close.
Hormuz remains effectively closed (tanker traffic standstill); Brent ~$84.5; gold ~$5,171; BTC ~$67.2k.
Assessment: legally open but effectively closed / near-standstill.
Hormuz remains legally open but effectively near-closed (very low transits) while oil stays >$100.
Hormuz effectively closed; oil elevated.
Hormuz remains effectively near-closed: commercial avoidance continues; new ship strike reporting.
Hormuz still severely disrupted but selectively passable; Brent still >$100.
Assessment: Effectively disrupted / near-standstill with selective passage.
Assessment: De-facto closed for most compliant operators; limited high-risk transits.
Hormuz remains effectively near-standstill; LNG shock persists (Qatar force majeure).
Fresh ship strikes reported; Hormuz technically open but operating like a wartime exclusion zone with near-zero commercial traffic.
Near-standstill shipping in Hormuz; multiple vessel attacks; oil spikes toward $100 Brent.
Tanker traffic still near-standstill; IEA agrees record 400M bbl oil reserve release; Brent ~$98, WTI ~$92.7.
Hormuz still severely disrupted; Brent back above $100 while Iran UN envoy says Tehran won't close the strait.
Hormuz still severely disrupted; Iran supreme leader says strait should remain shut while U.S. weighs coalition escorts.
Hormuz still severely disrupted (repeated ship attacks); Brent ~$103, gold ~$5,018 while LNG shock persists.
Hormuz transit still heavily suppressed; ceasefire efforts rejected; U.S. urges coalition warships for escorts.
Hormuz remains severely disrupted; U.S. Maritime Advisory warns of continued Iranian attacks as coalition escort planning continues.
JMIC: SoH threat level CRITICAL; observed traffic ~11 vessels/24h vs ~138/day historical; 21 incidents since 1 Mar.
Reuters: Strait described as nearly inaccessible; Iran-Oman protocol discussed to license/permit vessel transits; Iran parliament weighs banning “hostile” vessels and charging tolls.
CNBC: UN Security Council shipping-protection draft ("all necessary means") reportedly stalled; Iran continues curtailing tanker movements through Hormuz.

JMIC Update 025 (29 Mar): threat level remains CRITICAL; observed Strait of Hormuz traffic remains far below normal (~138/day), with 11 vessels observed in the last 24 hours (AIS-derived); no vessel attacks reported in past 72 hours.

Thailand: Fuel retailers raised pump prices Apr 3 (diesel +3.50 baht/litre; petrol/gasohol +0.70); exports +9.9% y/y in Feb but with widening trade deficit as imports +31.8%; Krungsri estimates Middle East escalation could cut Thailand GDP by 0.2–0.9pp via oil and logistics shocks.
